title: Kangaroo parametric ladder patch breaks the 200-point plateau
summary: A manual API-free Kangaroo patch separated low-level ladder-climb perception from route-selection exclusion. The retuned patch-2 policy improved high-budget Kangaroo evaluation from the previous stable 200.0 plateau to mean 2656.25 over 128 episodes with a 50000-step cap, but it is still exploratory and not yet a canonical covered-game policy.

context.previous_best: policy_v6 from scripts/llm_optimization/runs/single_game/kangaroo_resume3_20260430_144102 remained stuck at exactly 200.0 over 128 episodes with a 50000-step cap.
context.observed_failure: The debug overlay showed policy_v6 triggered UP from on_ladder_column_climb with only one pixel of player-ladder overlap, which caused repeated false climb behavior.
context.quentin_feedback_addressed: The follow-up used explicit parametric ladder predicates and debug traces showing action, logical decision stack, ladder overlap, and priority override reason.

changes.purpose: Keeps strict ladder-overlap perception while adding route_exclude_x_tol so route planning ignores ladders in the current x-column when choosing a dismount target.
Stores the local CMA-ES retuned parameters for the same patch-2 policy logic.
Updated the debug explanation path so the rendered trace understands the new route_exclude_x_tol parameter.

strict_patch1_result.result: The first strict overlap patch scored 0.0 over 32 episodes because it got stuck at the ladder top choosing RIGHT toward the same ladder column.

patch2_short_debug.result: 850-step diagnostic trace reached reward 200.0 with zero explanation mismatches.

retuned_debug.result: 850-step diagnostic trace reached reward 200.0, had zero explanation mismatches, and selected only RIGHT, LEFT, and RIGHTFIRE in the first 850 steps.

interpretation: This is the first Kangaroo evidence that clearly breaks the stable 200-point plateau under a large local evaluation budget.
The result supports Quentin's recommendation to expose intermediate predicates and tune their numeric thresholds instead of relying on a single hard-coded overlap rule.

The policy is still not a canonical solved Kangaroo policy. The short debug trace shows the first reward comes from horizontal routing plus punching, not from a clean demonstrated ladder-climbing sequence.
The remaining failures at 200.0 mean the policy is still brittle and should stay in exploratory Kangaroo artifacts until standardized 10000-step promotion criteria are defined and met.

rq_mapping.RQ1: Positive exploratory evidence that LeGPS-style interpretable code plus local parameter search can improve Kangaroo beyond the earlier plateau.
rq_mapping.RQ2: The debug trace remains necessary because higher score alone does not prove the policy uses intended ladder mechanics robustly.
rq_mapping.RQ4: The local CMA-ES retune gave a modest 32-episode improvement, supporting the role of numeric parameter search for perceptual thresholds.

risks: Manual patching can weaken the unified-method claim if presented as normal LeGPS output; it must be labeled as trace-guided exploratory repair.
The retuned high-budget mean is only slightly higher than the base patch-2 high-budget mean, so the retuned variant should be treated as current candidate, not definitive proof of robust improvement.
The policy still has 20 of 128 high-budget episodes at or near the old 200 plateau, so Kangaroo is not yet robustly solved.
recommended_next_steps: Render or trace longer successful retuned episodes to understand how the policy reaches 3600 and where 200-point failures diverge.
Keep patch-2 and retuned patch-2 separate from canonical best policies until a promotion protocol is chosen.
If continuing Kangaroo, focus on robust ladder-climbing perception and route-state decomposition rather than another broad LLM rewrite.
If prioritizing thesis coverage, freeze Kangaroo as exploratory progress and continue onboarding the next game while preserving these artifacts.
